calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant FCA US, LLC's motion to strike is OFF CALENDAR.

A party moving to strike a pleading or a part of a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] motion." (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(3).)

Moving Defendant here failed to demonstrate compliance with its meet-and-confer responsibilities. Moving Defendant reached out only by letter. (See Wang Decl. at paras. 4-5.) Sending a letter inviting the other side to initiate a conversation does not satisfy the rule. The clear legislative intent is that the conference involve personal interaction be conducted at a time that allows meaningful consideration of the other side's issues. The record does not support that Defendant met and conferred "in person, by telephone or by video conference." Defendant's motion, thus, is ordered off calendar.

Moving Defendant's time to respond to the complaint is extended by 20 days, running from the date of this order.

The court notes it does not have-and has no record of receiving-any courtesy copies papers filed in connection with this motion.

The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith before Defendant files the next pleading, unless that pleading is an answer.

Moving Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Moving Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant FCA US, LLC's Demurrer is OFF CALENDAR.

A party demurring to a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] demurrer." (Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41 (a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a)(3).)

Demurring Defendant here failed to demonstrate compliance with its meet-and-confer responsibilities. Demurring Defendant reached out only by letter. (See Wang Decl. at paras. 4-5.) Sending a letter inviting the other side to initiate a conversation does not satisfy the rule. The clear legislative intent is that the conference involve personal interaction be conducted at a time that allows meaningful consideration of the other side's issues. The record does not support that Defendant met and conferred "in person, by telephone or by video conference." Defendant's demurrer, thus, is ordered off calendar.

Demurring Defendant's time to respond to the complaint is extended by 20 days, running from the date of this order.

The court notes it does not have-and has no record of receiving-any courtesy copies papers filed in connection with this motion.

The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith before Defendant files the next pleading, unless that pleading is an answer.

Demurring Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Demurring Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Demurring Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2026 Set For Trial On Nov-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANT FCA US, LLC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendants FCA US, LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot Hilltop Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram's demurrer to the complaint is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendants' demurrer challenges Plaintiff's initial complaint, but Plaintiff has now filed an amended complaint. Thus, the court orders the instant demurer, which challenges a superseded pleading, off calendar.

Plaintiff is admonished regarding his meet and confer duties. In connection with demurrers and motions to strike, The Legislature clearly imposed upon both parties a duty to make a reasonable and good faith effort through personal communication to resolve the issues regarding the cross-complaint. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.41 and 435.5.) Not participating meaningfully is not an option and cannot be abided. Here, meaningful participation would have resulted in Plaintiff informing Defendants that he would be filing an amended complaint, sparing Defendants and the court from wasting resources on the demurrer and motion to strike.

Moving Parties are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving parties failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving parties are required to prepare proposed orders which repeat verbatim the court's ruling and must drop hard courtesy copies in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT FCA US, LLC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants FCA US, LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot Hilltop Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram's Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Complaint is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendants' motion challenges Plaintiff's initial complaint, but Plaintiff has now filed an amended complaint. Thus, the court orders the instant demurer, which challenges a superseded pleading, off calendar.

Plaintiff is admonished regarding his meet and confer duties. In connection with demurrers and motions to strike, The Legislature clearly imposed upon both parties a duty to make a reasonable and good faith effort through personal communication to resolve the issues regarding the cross-complaint. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.41 and 435.5.) Not participating meaningfully is not an option and cannot be abided. Here, meaningful participation would have resulted in Plaintiff informing Defendants that he would be filing an amended complaint, sparing Defendants and the court from wasting resources on the demurrer and motion to strike.

Moving Parties are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving parties failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving parties are required to prepare proposed orders which repeat verbatim the court's ruling and must drop hard courtesy copies in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
